title: Top 6 Claude Code Features to Get You Ahead of 99% of Users. (In Under 10 Minutes).
summary: A tutorial demonstrating six essential Claude Code features including connectors and automation capabilities. The video positions Claude Code as the most powerful part of Claude and teaches advanced usage techniques for building and enhancing projects with AI assistance.
writeup: A beginner-focused walkthrough of six core Claude Code features, demonstrated by building and automating a personal morning briefing dashboard that pulls live calendar, to-do, weather, and AI article data. The video moves from plan mode through MCP connector setup to cloud-hosted routines that run on a schedule without the local machine being on. The through-line is a concrete project: a 7 a.m. Gmail briefing that is fully operational by the end.
key_points: Plan mode prevents Claude from writing any code until the user approves a written plan first, and the presenter treats this as Claude Code's single most impactful feature for project quality.
Model choice (Opus 4.7, presented as the most capable option) and effort level (low through max) are set per session and directly control the depth and speed of the build.

MCP connectors link Claude Code to external services such as Google Calendar, Google Drive, Slack, and Gmail, enabling the dashboard to display the user's actual meetings and to-do items rather than placeholder data.
The /init slash command scans the current project and writes a claude.md file that preserves project-specific preferences and conventions across future sessions.
The Environments toggle switches execution from the local machine to Anthropic's cloud servers, which is a prerequisite for cloud-hosted routines.

Routines are scheduled prompts that run in the cloud on a cron-style schedule (the demo sets 7:00 a.m. daily) and can invoke connected tools like Gmail and Google Calendar even when the local computer is off; the presenter generates the routine prompt itself using Claude Code to maximize detail.
